channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Three Toronto Police Officers Arrested in Spain Suspended While Awaiting Investigation Details
item.description: Three off-duty Toronto police officers who were recently arrested in Spain have returned to Canada and are currently suspended with pay. The incident took place in Barcelona's Ciutat Vella district on...

item.title: Nigerian man extradited to US over alleged $1.5 million romance and fraud scheme
item.description: Samuel Ugberaese, wey people sabi as Putsammy or Samy, don get extradition from Nigeria go United States to face charges wey fit make am spend 40 years for prison. Dem dey accuse am say from 2014 reac...

item.title: Kalasha Awards winners still waiting for Sh8m prize money as Kenya Film Commission faces financial crunch
item.description: Seventeen days after the 2026 Kalasha Awards at KICC, Nairobi, several winners are yet to receive the Sh200,000 prize money for each of the 40 award categories, totaling Sh8 million. While President W...

item.title: Delhi MCD plans step-wise protocol for handling aggressive stray dogs following Supreme Court directions
item.description: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) is preparing to implement a cautious approach to managing aggressive stray dogs in the city, following the Supreme Court’s recent judgement prioritising human ...

item.title: The History and Legacy of Manchester's Peveril of the Peak Pub
item.description: Peveril of the Peak, affectionately called 'The Pev' by locals, is one of Manchester's most iconic Victorian-era pubs. Established around 1820, it is located on a distinctive triangular plot on Great ...

item.title: Chris Rabb Secures Democratic Nomination in Pennsylvania’s 3rd District
item.description: Chris Rabb, a progressive state representative known for his outspoken and activist style, won the Democratic primary for Pennsylvania’s 3rd Congressional District, a seat long held by Democrat Dwight...

item.title: APC Supporters Complain Over Disqualification of Longtime Party Member in Rivers Senatorial Primaries
item.description: Dem say for APC, some supporters don vex after former presidential aspirant, Mr Tein Jack-Rich, get disqualified from Rivers West Senatorial race for the upcoming 2027 elections. The coalition wey rep...

item.title: Severe Storms Hit Southwestern Ontario with At Least One Confirmed Tornado
item.description: Southwestern Ontario experienced significant damage on Tuesday as severe weather swept across the region, with at least one tornado reported. Environment Canada issued multiple tornado warnings for Br...

item.title: Examining the Authors Behind Trump’s Truth Social Posts and Political Influence
item.description: This podcast episode from The Guardian explores the inner workings of former US President Donald Trump's social media activity, particularly on his platform Truth Social. Hosts Reged Ahmad and the Gua...

item.title: Motorists Association of Kenya Rejects Partial Diesel Price Cut After Alleged Secret Talks with Government
item.description: The Motorists Association of Kenya (MAK) has strongly rejected a recent agreement reached between some transport sector leaders and the government on fuel prices. According to MAK, certain leaders wen...

item.title: Ricardo Goss acknowledges strong competition from Sipho Chaine and Brandon Petersen for Bafana Bafana World Cup goalkeeper selection
item.description: Bafana Bafana goalkeeper Ricardo Goss has openly admitted that the exceptional performances of fellow shot-stoppers Sipho Chaine and Brandon Petersen this season have made the battle for places in the...

item.title: Three sisters from London tragically die in Brighton sea
item.description: Three sisters from the Uxbridge area of London, Jane Adetoro, 36, Christina Walter, 32, and Rebecca Walter, 31, have tragically died in the sea off Brighton beach. The incident occurred near Black Roc...

item.title: Climate Conditions and Rodent Populations Linked to Hantavirus Outbreak in Argentina
item.description: The recent hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship highlights the growing public health risks associated with climate-driven ecological changes. In Argentina and Chile, wetter-than-usual con...

item.title: SDP faction under Gabam expels 13 party members and appoints new presidential candidate
item.description: Di Social Democratic Party (SDP) dey waka gbege as di faction wey dey follow Shehu Gabam don announce say dem don expel 13 important party members, including di party presidential candidate, Adewole A...

item.title: Arusha to Host Cape to Cairo International Marathon 2026
item.description: Tanzania is set to host the Cape to Cairo Arusha International Marathon in 2026, an event that is expected to attract runners and sports enthusiasts from across Africa and beyond. The marathon is not ...

item.title: Ranking of Top 10 Most Successful European Club Football Managers by Major Trophies
item.description: This article from Punch Newspaper highlight the top 10 managers wey don win the most European trophies for club football. At number one na Carlo Ancelotti, the Italian boss wey get five Champions Leag...

item.title: SpaceX lodges plans for potential trillion-dollar IPO
item.description: SpaceX, the space exploration company founded by Elon Musk, has formally lodged its IPO filing, signalling its intention to go public in what could be one of the most valuable market debuts in history...

item.title: Haryana Woman, Survivor of Sexual Assault, Dies by Suicide Amid Blackmail Threats
item.description: In a tragic incident from Nuh district, Haryana, a 20-year-old woman, who had earlier survived a gang rape, reportedly took her own life due to continuous blackmail and mental harassment. According to...

item.title: Thomas Massie Loses Kentucky Primary but Builds Potential Anti-Trump Faction Within GOP
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) lost his Republican primary election on May 19, 2026, to Trump-endorsed candidate Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL. The defeat highlights President Donald Trump's strong influ...

item.title: Above-normal temperatures prompt early heat warnings in the Maritimes
item.description: The Maritimes are experiencing unusually warm weather, with temperatures reaching 30°C in Halifax and 32°C in Fredericton, prompting the first heat warnings of the year in Nova Scotia. Residents are b...
